title: AI Accountability Specification
description: Schema for the AI Accountability Specification Standard — an engineering artifact for naming human ownership of AI decision boundaries before deployment. Standard version: v0.1.0.

systemName.description: The name of the AI system or component this specification covers.

systemVersion.description: The model version or deployment version this specification applies to.

date.description: Date this specification was created or last updated. ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DD.

specificationVersion.description: Version of the AI Accountability Specification Standard used to complete this document.

specificationOwner.description: The person responsible for the accuracy and currency of this specification document.

contact.description: Direct contact method — not a team inbox.

decisionBoundaries.description: The complete list of decision boundaries for this AI system. Every autonomous decision the system makes must be represented.

specificationHistory.description: Version history of this specification document.

decisionBoundary.title: Decision Boundary
decisionBoundary.description: A single decision boundary: the scope of autonomous AI action, who owns it, and what happens when it goes wrong.

id.description: Unique identifier for this boundary within the specification.

name.description: Short descriptive name for this decision boundary.

boundary.title: Decision Boundary Definition
boundary.description: Defines precisely what the AI system can do without human confirmation.

triggerCondition.description: The observable state or input that causes the system to act autonomously. Must be specific enough to be detectable in logs.

autonomousAction.description: What the system does when triggered, without human confirmation. Must be precise.

authorityLimit.description: The hard upper bound of what the system may do. Actions beyond this limit require human confirmation.

exclusionSet.description: Conditions under which the system must defer to a human even if the trigger condition is met.

namedOwner.title: Named Owner
namedOwner.description: The specific human being accountable for outcomes produced by this decision boundary.

fullName.description: Legal name as it appears in organizational records.

role.description: Current organizational role as it relates to this system.

contact.description: Direct, current contact method. Not a team inbox.

appointedBy.description: The person who formally designated this Named Owner.

dateAppointed.description: Date the Named Owner designation was made. ISO 8601 format.

acceptanceConfirmed.description: Named Owner has been notified and has confirmed acceptance in writing. Must be true for clearance.

consequenceChain.title: Consequence Chain
consequenceChain.description: What happens, in sequence, when this decision boundary is crossed incorrectly. Written before deployment.

immediateSystemResponse.description: What the system does automatically when a boundary violation is detected. Must be a defined, testable behavior.

method.description: How the Named Owner is notified (e.g., PagerDuty, SMS, email).

timeBoundMinutes.description: Maximum minutes before Named Owner must be notified.

notificationRecipients.description: Additional parties notified on a boundary violation, with their trigger conditions.

escalationPath.description: Ordered escalation steps if Named Owner does not respond.

triggerAfterMinutes.description: Minutes after Named Owner notification before this escalation step fires.

resolutionAuthority.description: The specific person who can declare the incident resolved and authorize boundary modifications.

reviewTrigger.description: Conditions under which a boundary violation triggers a formal review of the decision boundary itself.

tested.description: Whether the consequence chain has been tested before deployment clearance.

deploymentClearance.title: Deployment Clearance
deploymentClearance.description: The binary gate. All checklist items must be true for CLEARED status.

boundaryComplete.description: Decision boundary is complete: trigger condition, autonomous action, authority limit, and exclusion set all specified.

namedOwnerConfirmed.description: Named owner has been confirmed and has accepted designation in writing.

consequenceChainReviewed.description: Consequence chain has been reviewed and is complete.

clearedBy.description: The Clearance Authority. Must not be the Named Owner, Specification Owner, or primary system developer.

dateClearred.description: Date clearance was granted or denied.

status.description: Binary clearance status. CLEARED requires all checklist items to be true.

notClearedReason.description: If NOT_CLEARED: specific fields that are incomplete and the owner responsible for completing each.
